item.title: Cartoon by Dana Summers
item.description: Dana Summers cartoon on government shutdown, Congress, Democrats and Republicans.

item.title: This Trump administration tactic takes boycotts to the next level
item.description: Secondary boycotts are illegal for corporations and unions.

item.title: This invention to help older adults is so right, it seems obvious
item.description: A transformative tool is allowing family members to live better lives as they age.

item.title: How risky can the weight-loss drug boom be? I learned the hard way.
item.description: Unregulated copycat versions of GLP-1s are a dangerous blind spot.

item.title: A ‘post-human’ vision of AI is already causing problems
item.description: When firms speak of software as a moral agent, responsibility to actual people starts to erode.

item.title: Iran is not Venezuela, President Trump, and this man is no moderate
item.description: Seeking a shortcut out of Iran, the administration considers its Venezuela playbook.

item.title: Trump shrugs off an attack on U.S. lives. The reason has a name.
item.description: The president is just not bothered by anything Vladimir Putin does.

item.title: This $2 test leads to nearly 30,000 arrests a year for no reason
item.description: Innocent Americans are being sent to jail on drug charges because of false positives.

item.title: Down at the DMV, here’s the toughest test of all
item.description: Departments of motor vehicles are where the public sees government in action ... or inaction.

item.title: Special counseling
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-New York), campaign funds, psychiatrist.

item.title: The Supreme Court’s birthright citizenship options
item.description: The justices could make a sweeping ruling or a narrower one.

item.title: Three cautionary tales from the Iran energy shock
item.description: Never confuse good intentions with good planning.

item.title: FDR was our grandfather. DHS is betraying his vision.
item.description: Border wall expansion, national park preservation and TSA tips, in readers’ eyes.

item.title: An embarrassing ritual plagues the NBA. The solution is math.
item.description: Teams are deliberately tanking to game the draft. It doesn’t have to be this way.

item.title: From lobster to laptops, organized crime is targeting U.S. freight
item.description: Cargo theft costs the U.S. trucking industry a staggering $18 million every day.

item.title: The government’s DEI complex lives on
item.description: A new lawsuit challenges the race requirement in an HHS medical scholarship.

item.title: These 1960s orders trap Alabama schools in the past
item.description: Alabama schools have long desegregated. But these court orders still mandate racial quotas.

item.title: Why is my kindergartner watching ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ in school?
item.description: In D.C. schools, even the youngest pupils regularly use tablets and other electronic devices in class.

item.title: Ukraine’s drone army has done the incredible
item.description: Russia hoped winter would bring Ukraine to its knees. Here’s why it didn’t.

item.title: Progressive capitalism for the post-Trump era
item.description: Too many Americans have been economically left behind. Democrats, embrace inclusive prosperity.

item.title: Data centers and a debate worth having
item.description: Data centers, ageism, National Mall bike lanes, Social Security and more, in readers eyes.
